items.title: Scientists discover strange “narwhal” waves that trap light beyond known limits
items.summary: Physicists at Peking University have uncovered a new way to confine light far beyond conventional limits — without relying on metals and their inherent energy dissipation. By formulating the singular dispersion equation, the team discovered narwhal-shaped wavefunctions that trap light at deep-subwav

items.title: Scientists discover the nutrient that can supercharge cellular energy
items.summary: Researchers discovered that leucine, a nutrient found in protein-rich foods, can supercharge mitochondria by protecting crucial energy-producing proteins inside cells. The breakthrough uncovers a powerful new link between diet and cellular energy — with possible implications for cancer and metabolic

items.title: NASA Wins Four Telly Awards for Artemis Moon Coverage, More
items.summary: NASA’s historic Artemis II mission coverage, which connected global audiences to watch the first humans to travel around the Moon in more than half a century, is among the agency’s video productions recognized with four 2026 Telly Awards. “President Trump’s leadership in establishing the Artemis pro

items.title: UNESCO warns a tsunami in the Mediterranean is inevitable
items.summary: The French Riviera may look like an unlikely place for a tsunami disaster, but scientists warn the threat is far more real than most people realize. Historical events and new modeling show that destructive waves have already struck the Mediterranean coast — and could hit again with very little warni

items.title: Scientists discover a strange “inside-out” planetary system that shouldn’t exist
items.summary: Scientists have discovered a bizarre planetary system where a rocky world orbits farther out than giant gas planets, defying long-standing theories of planet formation.
